texto: A five-hour flight works out to three days and nights on land, by rail, from sea to shining sea. You can chalk off the hours on the back of the seat ahead. But seventy-some hours will not seem so long to you if you tell yourself first: This is where I am going to be for the rest of my natural life.
explicacao: Citação de Amy Hempel sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “A five-hour flight works out to three days and nights on land, by rail, from sea to shining sea. …” articula reflexao, moving, page-151 em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e viagem.

texto: consolation is a beautiful word. everyone skins his knee-that doesnt make yours hurt anyless.

texto: There’s so much I can’t read because I get so exasperated. Someone starts describing the character boarding the plane and pulling the seat back. And I just want to say, Babe, I have been downtown. I have been up in a plane. Give me some credit.

texto: I think of the chimp, the one with the talking hands. In the course of the experiment, that chimp had a baby. Imagine how her trainers must have thrilled when the mother, without prompting, began to sign her newborn. Baby, drink milk. Baby, play ball. And when the baby died, the mother stood over the body, her wrinkled hands moving with animal grace, forming again and again the words: Baby, come hug, Baby come hug, fluent now in the language of grief.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Amy Hempel sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “I think of the chimp, the one with the talking hands. In the course of the experiment, that chimp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, grief, chimp, útil em momentos de luto e dia-chuvoso.

texto: I had my own bed. I slept in it alone, except for those times when we needed—not sex—but sex was how we got there.
explicacao: Citação de Amy Hempel sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “I had my own bed. I slept in it alone, except for those times when we needed—not sex—but sex was …” articula amor, love, lovers em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como casamento e lovers.

texto: The worst of it is over now, and I can't say that I am glad. Lose that sense of loss—you have gone and lost something else. But the body moves toward health. The mind, too, in steps. One step at a time. Ask a mother who has just lost a child, How many children do you have? "Four," she will say, "—three," and years later, "Three," she will say, "—four.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Amy Hempel que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “The worst of it is over now, and I can't say that I am glad.
